Steal a Brainrot: Auto Steal, Instant Steal, Invisible.", "relevance_score": 0.6}, {"title": "Investing.com - StockMarketQuotes & Financial News", "url": "https://www.investing.com/", "snippet": "[DDG Snippet] Real-time quotes, charts, news & tools from Investing.com. Get AI analysis & premium data with InvestingPro to uncover strategicmarketopportunities.Recently Released Key EconomicEvents.", "relevance_score": 0.6}, {"title": "How to calculate your total addressablemarket... - For Entrepreneurs", "url": "https://www.forentrepreneurs.com/calculating-tam/", "snippet": "[Jina Parallel Content] Title: How to calculate your total addressable market and make a great TAM slide for investors URL Source: https://www.forentrepreneurs.com/calculating-tam/ Published Time: 2017-12-06T18:52:42+00:00 Markdown Content: I\u2019ve seen hundreds of startup pitch decks in my time at Matrix, and have found total addressable market (TAM) slides to be among the most frequently mis-executed. I get the sense they are often included as a formality in an attempt to get VCs to check a mental box and continue on hearing about other important things: the product, the team, the progress, the go-to-market, etc. In this piece, I\u2019m going to walk through the importance of thinking through TAM in a structured way, and present some ideas on how a more thoughtful TAM analysis and slide can improve a startup\u2019s pitch as well as its business decisions. ### What is TAM in the first place? To answer this question, we need to start with what a TAM analysis is for. Simply put, investors (and smart management teams) use TAM analyses in an attempt to identify gating factors on the growth of a startup over time. At Matrix, we\u2019re transparently looking to invest in companies that can one day become billion dollar businesses, and we see many pitches where the founder simply isn\u2019t targeting a market big enough to get there and doesn\u2019t have a plan to do so. A TAM slide\u2019s role in a pitch deck is to convince investors that the company is chasing an opportunity big enough to achieve venture-scale returns with the right execution. In the very best pitches, founders use their TAM slides to show insights into how they think about the market they are serving, the extent of their product-market-fit and their product roadmap. The goal of this piece is to help build a slide that accomplishes these goals and elevates the TAM conversation from a throwaway slide to a positive contributor to the pitch and discussions that follow. ### Three ways to calculate TAM There are three distinct ways to calculate TAM: 1.   Top-down, using industry research and reports. 2.   Bottom-up, using data from early selling efforts. 3.   Value theory, using conjecture about buyer willingness to pay. Let\u2019s discuss each in turn, using examples to help keep things relatable. **1) Top down,**which takes the form of\u201caccording to Gartner, this is a $Xbn market by 2022\u201d or \u201cwe serve potato farmers, a $40bn industry.\u201d This is by far my least favorite way to frame a TAM discussion. It contains remarkably little information, and relies on my faith in the analysts at Gartner. They\u2019re smart people, but market sizing is hard and often reliant on self-reported data or difficult-to-make estimates about the revenue of private companies. In cases where there are mixed pricing models (consider a software segment transitioning from license to SaaS pricing), the analysis can be confounded further. When a founder presents a number taken from a Gartner report, the default presumption is that they did some Googling in the midst of the deck-build", "relevance_score": 0.85}, {"title": "Identifying YourTargetMarket- Mautic", "url": "https://mautic.org/blog/identifying-target-market/", "snippet": "[DDG Snippet] Identifying YourTargetMarket. dbhurley. June 11, 2018.Sometimes you have to get creative. And this is the point of the post. You can\u2019t always rely on the standardtargetmarketmeasurements when attempting to identify your audience.", "relevance_score": 0.5}, {"title": "TargetFieldSection119 | TikTok", "url": "https://www.tiktok.com/discover/target-field-section-119", "snippet": "[DDG Snippet] TargetField Legend's Landing, all-you-can-eat seats, unlimited concessions, Pepsi products, chips, chicken tenders, hot dogs, Legend's Landing tickets, Minnesota Twins,TargetField food, foodoptionsatTargetField, Legend's Landing menu, food experience atTargetField.", "relevance_score": 0.5}]
